text:
What's good to eat at Augie? The dining hall in the Gerber Center is one of four options on campus where you can get food. The other areas include Gus's Snack Bar in the Brunner Theatre Center,
 the Westerlin Market in the Westerlin Dorm, and the Brew by the Slough in the Gerber Center. All of those options allow you to use your meal swipes or pay with cash. If you're unsure of what meal plan to get, check out the dining hall website.
You'll find information about meal plans, hours, calendars, and catering. You have endless possibilities and unique options for meals throughout the week. My favorites are Mondays, Fridays, and Sundays in the dining hall and Wednesdays
 at Gus's Snack Bar.

unique options for meals throughout the week. My favorites are Mondays, Fridays, and Sundays in the dining hall and Wednesdays
 at Gus's Snack Bar. On Monday nights, it's breakfast for dinner. Students can enjoy sausage links, scrambled eggs, and hashbrowns. Fridays are Fresh Fruit Fridays. The dining hall offers strawberries, blueberries, bananas, apples, and other fruits with lunch. It is popular with students
and staff. On Sundays, the Gerber dining hall offers a build-your-own sundae bar with ice cream and toppings. The dining hall also serves a variety of meals throughout the week, including a mac and cheese bar. The mac and cheese bar is described as one of the most popular dining options.

variety of meals throughout the week, including a mac and cheese bar. The mac and cheese bar is described as one of the most popular dining options. On Wednesdays, Gus's Snack Bar serves Walking Tacos. Students can choose Fritos or Doritos as a base and add toppings such as beans, taco-seasoned beef, lettuce,
tomatoes, olives, and salsa. The dining areas provide both food and a social atmosphere where students can eat with friends or on their own.

Eating in the dining hall Dining Regular dining hours (fall and spring semesters) Gerber Dining Center: 7 a.m.-8 p.m. Monday-Thursday; 7 a.m.-7 p.m. Friday; 9 a.m.-7 p.m. Saturday; and 9 a.m.-8 p.m. Sunday. Brew by the Slough: 7:30 a.m.-8 p.m. Monday-Thursday; 7:30 a.m.-5 p.m. Friday; 11 a.m.-3 p.m. Saturday and Sunday. Westerlin Market: 2 p.m.-midnight Monday-Thursday and Sunday; and 2-8 p.m. Friday and Saturday. Gus's Snack Bar: 9:30 a.m.-2:30 p.m. weekdays. Whether you want to enjoy a sit-down dinner with friends or grab a quick burger on your way to class, you'll find what you want to eat at Augustana. Locations

want to enjoy a sit-down dinner with friends or grab a quick burger on your way to class, you'll find what you want to eat at Augustana. Locations Augustana's main dining hall in The Gerber Center for Student Life has individual marché stations to serve fresh, fast and made-to-order options. Choose from
Chinese, Indian, Italian and Mexican food. Also vegetarian, soups and salads, seafood, burgers and sandwiches, desserts - and a variety of other dishes, depending
on the day. The Gerber Center dining room offers a four-week rotating menu. These menus are created at the beginning of each academic year by our talented staff. Daily menus accommodate preferences and requirements for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free and Halal foods.

of each academic year by our talented staff. Daily menus accommodate preferences and requirements for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free and Halal foods. Dining services also uses produce and meats from our on-campus Augie Acres and local farms, uses biodegradable containers, and partners with local growers to
 compost food waste. Another great part about the dining center? It's right upstairs from the Tredway Library and the Learning Commons, so you can break for a meal in the middle of
your studies. If you're in the Gerber Center and just need a quick latté and bagel, you'll find pastries, grab-and-go food and coffee drinks (and an outdoor patio) in
the Brew by the Slough. The Brew also provides a great setting for live performances by students and local musicians and comedians.

outdoor patio) in
the Brew by the Slough. The Brew also provides a great setting for live performances by students and local musicians and comedians. The Westerlin Market in Westerlin Residence Hall sells snacks and other quick needs every day and night of the week, and Gus' Snack Bar offers sandwiches and
lighter fare for students hanging out in the Brunner Theatre Center, or just passing through.

Dining Locations and Hours Gerber Center Dining Room The Gerber Center Dining Room offers casual dining at six food stations with different cuisines, multiple beverage stations and the Breakfast Bar. It is located
on the fifth floor of the Gerber Center for Student Life. This is an all-you-care-to-eat facility. Breakfast is $10; lunch is $11.50; and dinner is $11.50. Carryout meals are the same price. The marketplace style dining stations are: American Grill, featuring grilled sandwiches, quesadillas, fries, Viking Subs and Burgers. The Carvery, with classic favorites and chef specialties. Wild Thymes, offering vegetarian and vegan options. Green Creations, with sandwiches, wraps, soups, a salad bar, and fruit and yogurt bar. Global Fusion, specializing in international foods.

options. Green Creations, with sandwiches, wraps, soups, a salad bar, and fruit and yogurt bar. Global Fusion, specializing in international foods. Bella Luna, offering Italian cuisine including fresh-baked pizzas and made-to-order pasta dishes. Söt, featuring pastries and desserts from the campus bakery. The Breakfast Bar is open all day and includes cereals, bagels, muffins, waffles, and more. Beverage stations offer juices, milk, sodas, coffee, and tea. Dining Services hosts special events including Fresh Fruit Friday, Midnight Breakfast, and holiday menus throughout the year. Hours Monday-Thursday:
Continuous service 7 a.m.-8 p.m.
Breakfast: 7-10:30 a.m.
Lunch: 10:30 a.m.-1:30 p.m.
Dinner: 4:30-8 p.m.

and walking tacos. Hours:
Monday-Friday: 9:30 a.m.-2:30 p.m. Meal Bundles Available at all locations except the Gerber Dining Center. Includes one entrée, two sides or chips, and a drink. Entrées include sandwiches, salads, wraps, parfaits, and veggie or fruit trays. Sides include pasta salad, cottage cheese, carrots, apple slices, fruit, cheese,
or chips. Drinks may be canned or bottled.

Dining FAQ for Students What is a meal plan? A meal plan is a pre-paid account for on-campus dining purchases. Meal plans are set up at the beginning of each academic term and charged to the student's tuition
bill. Meal plans include a set number of meals and Viking Bucks loaded onto the student ID card. Meal plans are non-transferable and cannot be used by others. Students must show photo ID when using their meal plan. Breakfast is 7-10:30 a.m., lunch is 10:30 a.m.-4:30 p.m., and dinner is 4:30-8 p.m. Students can use up to two meal swipes per meal period. What is a meal swipe? Meal swipes can be used at the all-you-can-eat Gerber Center Dining Room or to purchase meal bundles at Gus' Snack Bar, Brew by the Slough, and Westerlin Market.

can be used at the all-you-can-eat Gerber Center Dining Room or to purchase meal bundles at Gus' Snack Bar, Brew by the Slough, and Westerlin Market. Meal swipes on the Any 80 and Any 100 meal plans roll over term-to-term during the academic year. Any unused meal swipes at the end of the academic year are
forfeited. What are Viking Bucks? Viking Bucks are valued dollar-for-dollar and can be used at any Dining Services location on campus, including the Gerber Center Dining Room, Brew by the Slough,
Westerlin Market, Gus' Snack Bar, and concession stands. Viking Bucks roll over term-to-term during the academic year. Any remaining Viking Bucks at the end of the academic year are forfeited. Who is required to have a meal plan?

term-to-term during the academic year. Any remaining Viking Bucks at the end of the academic year are forfeited. Who is required to have a meal plan? All first-year students and sophomores are required to have an on-campus meal plan. Juniors, seniors, and commuter students may choose whether to purchase a meal
plan. How do I sign up for a meal plan? First-year students and sophomores are automatically assigned the Any 15 plan. Meal plan changes must be made during the first two weeks of the term. How can I check my meal plan account balance? Students can ask a cashier at any dining location or contact the Dining Services Office. Can I add additional Viking Bucks or meals? Additional Viking Bucks or meals may be added at any time through the Dining Services Office.

Office. Can I add additional Viking Bucks or meals? Additional Viking Bucks or meals may be added at any time through the Dining Services Office. What happens to unused meals and Viking Bucks? Unused Viking Bucks and off-campus meal plan swipes roll over during the academic year but are forfeited at the end of the academic year. No refunds are given. How much do meals cost at the Gerber Center Dining Room? Breakfast (7-10:30 a.m.): $10.25 Lunch (10:30 a.m.-4:30 p.m.): $11.75 Dinner (4:30-8 p.m.): $11.75 Meals are all-you-can-eat. Carryout meals cost the same price. What is continuous dining? Continuous dining means hot meals are available throughout operating hours. Is carryout available?

cost the same price. What is continuous dining? Continuous dining means hot meals are available throughout operating hours. Is carryout available? Students may create a to-go meal using a meal swipe, Viking Bucks, cash, credit/debit cards, or checks. One to-go container is included with on-campus meal plans for first-year and transfer students. Additional containers may be purchased for $6. How do I get a sick meal? Students who cannot visit the dining hall may arrange for a friend to pick up a meal by contacting the Dining Services Office and providing their information. What is a meal bundle? Meal bundles generally include one entrée, two side dishes, and a drink. Bundles are available at Westerlin Market, Gus' Snack Bar, and Brew by the Slough.

generally include one entrée, two side dishes, and a drink. Bundles are available at Westerlin Market, Gus' Snack Bar, and Brew by the Slough. Entrées may include sandwiches, wraps, salads, yogurt parfaits, fruit trays, vegetable trays, and hot grilled items. Side dishes may include pasta salad, cottage cheese, carrots, apple slices, fresh fruit, cheese, and chips. Drinks include soda, bottled water, or milk. Can friends join me in the dining room if they are not eating? Only people who have purchased a meal may enter the dining room. Where is the Dining Services Office? The office is located in the Dining Room and is open Monday-Friday from 8 a.m.-4:30 p.m. What if I have special dietary needs?

Services Office? The office is located in the Dining Room and is open Monday-Friday from 8 a.m.-4:30 p.m. What if I have special dietary needs? Dining locations offer gluten-free, vegetarian, and vegan options daily. Students may contact the Dining Services Office for additional assistance.

Dining options at Augie The Gerber Center Dining Room is the main dining hall on campus, located on the fifth floor of the Gerber Center for Student Life. It is
all-you-care-to-eat and includes stations like American Grill, Carvery, Global Fusion, Bella Luna, Green Creations, Wild Thymes, Söt bakery,
and an all-day Breakfast Bar. Meals are served daily with rotating hours: weekday breakfast, lunch, and dinner, plus brunch on weekends. Gävle Grab&Go dining room offers quick meals and seating, serving lunch and dinner on weekdays and lunch on Friday. Westerlin Market is a convenience store in Westerlin Residence Hall with snacks, drinks, frozen meals, and essentials. It is open daily
with extended evening hours on weekdays.

store in Westerlin Residence Hall with snacks, drinks, frozen meals, and essentials. It is open daily
with extended evening hours on weekdays. Brew by the Slough is a café in the Gerber Center offering coffee drinks, pastries, soups, and grab-and-go food. It is open mornings through
evening on weekdays and shorter weekend hours. Gus' Snack Bar is in the Brunner Theater Center and serves snacks plus made-to-order items like burgers and walking tacos, open weekdays
during lunch hours. Meal bundles are available at most locations and include an entrée, two sides or chips, and a drink.

Augie Dining Menu Schedule The Augustana Dining Menu Schedule is a digital web-based interface designed to help students and users browse, filter, and search dining options across campus in real
time. Instead of displaying a static list of meals, the system functions as a dynamic database viewer where menu items are organized by date, dining location, meal
period, and dietary category. At the top of the page, the system title "AUGUSTANA DINING MENU SCHEDULE" is displayed along with a QR code. This QR code allows users to quickly access the page on a
mobile device, making it easy to check menus while on campus. Users are encouraged to scan the QR code and bookmark the page for repeated access. The interface is
designed to be mobile-friendly and supports frequent use throughout the week.

the QR code and bookmark the page for repeated access. The interface is
designed to be mobile-friendly and supports frequent use throughout the week. Below the header, the system provides a set of instructions explaining how to interact with the menu tool. Users can search for specific food items using a search bar
(for example, typing "pizza" to see all pizza-related items). The system also supports date-based queries, where users can enter a specific date in month/day format
(such as 06/15) to view menu items available on that day only. This makes it possible to quickly locate what is being served without browsing the entire menu manually.

view menu items available on that day only. This makes it possible to quickly locate what is being served without browsing the entire menu manually. The system also includes functionality to export the currently viewed menu into a CSV file. This allows users to download the data and open it in spreadsheet software
such as Excel for further organization or planning. This feature is especially useful for users who want to track meals over time or analyze dining patterns. A key feature of the interface is visual highlighting: menu items for the current day are marked in blue. This helps users immediately distinguish today's offerings
from other dates in the dataset, improving readability and navigation efficiency.

in blue. This helps users immediately distinguish today's offerings
from other dates in the dataset, improving readability and navigation efficiency. Under the "Quick Filter Buttons" section, users are provided with preset navigation options. These include time-based filters such as "Today," "This Week," and
"Next Week," which automatically adjust the visible menu data. Additional meal-type filters include "Breakfast," "Lunch," "Dinner," and "Brunch," allowing users to narrow results based on meal periods. These filters reduce the need for manual searching and improve usability for frequent users.

allowing users to narrow results based on meal periods. These filters reduce the need for manual searching and improve usability for frequent users. The system also supports dietary and allergen-based filtering. Users can toggle options such as Vegetarian (VGT), Gluten Free (GF), Vegan (V), Shellfish Alert, Tree
Nut Alert, and Peanut Alert. These filters allow users with dietary restrictions or preferences to quickly identify safe food options without manually scanning each
menu entry. A keyword search bar is also provided below the filters, enabling users to search the full dataset for specific terms. This is useful for locating individual menu items,
ingredients, or cuisine types across multiple dining locations and dates.

for specific terms. This is useful for locating individual menu items,
ingredients, or cuisine types across multiple dining locations and dates. The main data table area beneath the controls is structured by date and menu entries. Each row corresponds to a specific dining station, meal type, and menu item.
However, in the current view shown, the table displays "No data," indicating that no menu entries are currently loaded or that a filter selection has not returned results. Overall, this system serves as a structured navigation and filtering tool rather than a static menu list. It organizes dining data into a searchable and filterable
format, enabling users to efficiently find food options across multiple campus dining locations, dates, and dietary categories.

What is dining like at Augustana? The dining system at Augustana includes several on-campus locations designed to provide both full meals and quick food options throughout the day. Main dining areas include the Gerber Dining
Center, Brew by the Slough, Westerlin Market, and Gus's Snack Bar, each serving different schedules and food styles.

areas include the Gerber Dining
Center, Brew by the Slough, Westerlin Market, and Gus's Snack Bar, each serving different schedules and food styles. Regular dining hours vary by location. The Gerber Dining Center is open 7 a.m.-8 p.m. Monday through Thursday, 7 a.m.-7 p.m. on Friday, 9 a.m.-7 p.m. on Saturday, and 9 a.m.-8 p.m. on Sunday.
Brew by the Slough operates 7:30 a.m.-8 p.m. Monday through Thursday, 7:30 a.m.-5 p.m. on Friday, and 11 a.m.-3 p.m. on weekends. Westerlin Market is open late, from 2 p.m.-midnight Monday
through Thursday and Sunday, and 2-8 p.m. on Friday and Saturday. Gus's Snack Bar is open weekdays from 9:30 a.m.-2:30 p.m.

2 p.m.-midnight Monday
through Thursday and Sunday, and 2-8 p.m. on Friday and Saturday. Gus's Snack Bar is open weekdays from 9:30 a.m.-2:30 p.m. The Gerber Dining Center is the main dining hall located in the Gerber Center for Student Life. It features multiple made-to-order stations offering cuisines such as Chinese, Indian, Italian,
and Mexican food, along with salads, soups, seafood, burgers, sandwiches, and desserts. The menu rotates every four weeks and changes daily. It also accommodates dietary needs including
vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and Halal options. Dining services emphasize sustainability by sourcing produce and meat from on-campus Augie Acres and local farms, using biodegradable containers, and partnering with local growers to compost
food waste.

produce and meat from on-campus Augie Acres and local farms, using biodegradable containers, and partnering with local growers to compost
food waste. Brew by the Slough offers coffee drinks, pastries, and grab-and-go food in a café-style space with an outdoor patio. It also hosts student performances and local entertainment. Westerlin Market, located in the residence hall, provides snacks and convenience items throughout the day and night, making it a late-access option for students. Gus's Snack Bar offers lighter meals such as sandwiches and quick bites for students in or near the Brunner Theatre Center. Overall, Augustana dining is structured to give students flexible access to meals, from full dining hall experiences to quick snacks and late-night options across campus.

What are the meal plans for 2025-26 at Augustana? Meal plans at Augustana are prepaid dining accounts used for on-campus food purchases. They are set up at the start of each academic term and charged to a student's tuition bill. Each plan
includes a set number of meal swipes plus Viking Bucks, which are loaded onto a student ID card and automatically deducted when used. First-year and sophomore students are automatically enrolled in the Any 15 plan each semester. This plan provides 15 meals per week and includes Viking Bucks. Students can change their plan
during the first two weeks of the semester through the housing and dining portal or by contacting Dining Services. Junior and senior students are not automatically assigned a meal plan but can purchase any available option at any time.

contacting Dining Services. Junior and senior students are not automatically assigned a meal plan but can purchase any available option at any time. Meal plans can be used across campus dining locations, including the Gerber Dining Center, Brew by the Slough, Westerlin Market, and Gus's Snack Bar. However, the Gerber Unlimited plan is
restricted to meal swipes used only in the Gerber Dining Center.

Slough, Westerlin Market, and Gus's Snack Bar. However, the Gerber Unlimited plan is
restricted to meal swipes used only in the Gerber Dining Center. There are multiple plan types depending on how often students eat on campus. The Gerber Unlimited plan provides unlimited access to the dining hall plus Viking Bucks and retail meal swipes for
other locations. Other plans such as Any 19, Any 15, Any 12, and Any 10 provide a fixed number of meals per week along with varying amounts of Viking Bucks. Some off-campus plans like Any 80
and Any 100 provide a fixed number of meals per term rather than per week.

with varying amounts of Viking Bucks. Some off-campus plans like Any 80
and Any 100 provide a fixed number of meals per term rather than per week. Meal swipes can be used for dine-in meals or certain to-go options, and most plans allow up to four swipes per day. Weekly meal plans reset each Sunday and do not roll over unused meals.
Semester-based plans and Viking Bucks may roll over depending on the plan, but all unused balances expire at the end of the academic year. Viking Bucks function like campus cash and can be used in all dining locations and concessions. They can also be reloaded at a dollar-for-dollar rate, with a 10% bonus when purchasing $500 or
more at once. Unused Viking Bucks generally roll over during the academic year but expire after commencement.

The dining hall at Augustana is the central dining option on campus and is part of a broader system that includes several other food locations such as Brew by the Slough, Westerlin Market,
and Gus's Snack Bar. Together, these locations give students multiple ways to access food throughout the day, whether they want a full sit-down meal or something quick on the go. The dining hall itself is generally described by students as a consistent and dependable place to eat. It is not viewed as restaurant-quality dining, but rather as a practical and reliable
option that meets everyday student needs. Most students agree that it provides enough variety and quantity of food that it is easy to find something to eat at any time during the week.

needs. Most students agree that it provides enough variety and quantity of food that it is easy to find something to eat at any time during the week. One of the main features of the dining hall is its variety of food stations. These stations rotate regularly and include different types of cuisine, such as international-inspired dishes
alongside more standard American college dining options. Students often mention that having multiple stations helps prevent the food from feeling repetitive, since different meals are offered
throughout the day and across the week.

having multiple stations helps prevent the food from feeling repetitive, since different meals are offered
throughout the day and across the week. The dining hall also includes options for students with different dietary needs. Vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly meals are commonly available, and there are usually choices that
accommodate different preferences or restrictions. This flexibility is one of the reasons students describe the dining hall as accessible and inclusive for a wide range of diets.

or restrictions. This flexibility is one of the reasons students describe the dining hall as accessible and inclusive for a wide range of diets. Another commonly mentioned aspect is the presence of occasional themed or rotating meal offerings. While not guaranteed every day, these variations help add interest to the dining experience
and give students something different from the regular daily menu. Some students note that international-style dishes can sometimes be found, adding more diversity compared to typical college
dining halls.

menu. Some students note that international-style dishes can sometimes be found, adding more diversity compared to typical college
dining halls. In terms of experience, students generally view the dining hall as a social space as well as a place to eat. It is a common meeting point where students gather between classes, eat with friends,
 or take breaks during study sessions. Its location near academic buildings also makes it convenient for students who need quick access to food during the day.

during study sessions. Its location near academic buildings also makes it convenient for students who need quick access to food during the day. Overall, the dining hall is described as practical, consistent, and sufficient for student life. While it is not considered exceptional or gourmet, it fulfills its role effectively by providing
steady meal options, flexible dietary choices, and enough variety to support everyday campus life without major issues.

This Reddit thread is discussing whether there are any red flags about attending Augustana College, with a focus on academics, computer science, career outcomes, and overall student experience. The original poster is mainly concerned about whether paying around $23k-$24k per year is worth it, especially for a Computer Science major, and whether the school provides strong internship
opportunities, good employment outcomes, and a fun college experience.

a Computer Science major, and whether the school provides strong internship
opportunities, good employment outcomes, and a fun college experience. Several responses suggest that there are **no major red flags**. Students and commenters mention that Augustana generally has good student support, active campus life, and decent academic
resources. One common point is that the school provides opportunities such as funding for internships or study abroad experiences, which can help with career development and hands-on experience
outside the classroom.

such as funding for internships or study abroad experiences, which can help with career development and hands-on experience
outside the classroom. For Computer Science specifically, the sentiment is that while it may not compete with top-tier research universities, it is considered solid for a liberal arts college. The emphasis is more
on accessible professors, smaller class sizes, and personalized attention rather than large-scale research output. In terms of employment and internships, commenters are generally optimistic. Some mention that students are able to find internships and jobs, especially with support from campus resources and
the broader demand for tech workers. The overall expectation is that students who actively seek opportunities tend to do well after graduation.

and
the broader demand for tech workers. The overall expectation is that students who actively seek opportunities tend to do well after graduation. There is also discussion about campus life being a positive aspect of the school. Students describe it as having a good social scene, with events, activities, and opportunities to get involved.
This contributes to a more enjoyable overall college experience. However, there is some disagreement in the thread. A minority opinion raises concerns about location and safety, describing Rock Island negatively and claiming limited opportunities. This
viewpoint is strongly contradicted by other commenters, who say the area is generally safe with normal urban precautions and that students regularly have positive experiences living there.

commenters, who say the area is generally safe with normal urban precautions and that students regularly have positive experiences living there. Overall, the thread does not identify any consistent "red flags." The general conclusion from most responses is that Augustana is a solid choice for students who value small class sizes,
supportive faculty, and an active campus environment, especially if they are proactive about internships and career planning.
